codeReview.summary: Implementation adds TTS playback controls (VOLUME_UP play button) to AI message bubbles in chat UI. The audio player is properly initialized with lazy loading, state changes are handled correctly, and the play button only appears on Marcus (AI) messages, not user messages. Tests cover the key behaviors including initialization, button creation, and VAL-SPEECH-002 requirements. Code passes lint and typecheck.

issues.description: Test uses hasattr() instead of isinstance() for Mock compatibility. Works correctly but slightly unconventional pattern compared to typical Flet testing.

ft.Audio is deprecated since Flet 0.26.0 and will be removed in 0.29.0. The code uses the deprecated class but this is consistent with existing codebase patterns (ft.AudioRecorder also deprecated). Consider migrating to flet-audio package in future.
The synthesize_speech method has no test coverage in api_client tests. While session_page tests mock the API call, integration tests for the api_client method itself would strengthen the test suite.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature fulfills VAL-SPEECH-001 through VAL-SPEECH-004 according to the commit message. However, VAL-SPEECH-001 (microphone button visible) and VAL-SPEECH-003 (recording indicator shows) were already implemented in a prior commit (e781422 - faster-whisper STT integration). The current commit only adds TTS playback controls. This feature scope is correctly focused on UI controls but the fulfills array references pre-existing behaviors.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Commit e781422 added mic_button and recording_indicator to session_page.py. Commit 11628d8 only adds audio_player, _play_tts, and _get_play_button.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The flet-worker skill instructs to verify implementation by running 'flet run' interactively. The worker instead ran pytest tests which provided automated verification. This is a valid alternative given the skill's flexibility in verification step.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows tests ran via pytest, no interactive flet run verification documented.

summary: Feature speech-ui-controls passes review. TTS play button (VOLUME_UP) correctly appears on AI (Marcus) message bubbles only. Audio player initialization is lazy, state handling is correct, and 7 new tests verify the behavior. Code passes ruff lint and mypy typecheck. Minor notes: Flet Audio deprecation warning (pre-existing pattern), and synthesize_speech api_client method lacks unit tests (but is exercised via session_page integration tests).
